# Quick Start Guide

## Running RuneScript Programs

### Prerequisites
- Java 21 or higher installed on your system

### Quick Test
1. Save this code as `hello.rn`:
```runescript
print("Hello, RuneScript!");
```

2. Run it:
```bash
java -jar runescript.jar hello.rn
```

### Available Commands
```bash
# Execute a script
java -jar runescript.jar script.rn

# View tokenization
java -jar runescript.jar --emit-tokens script.rn

# View Abstract Syntax Tree
java -jar runescript.jar --emit-ast script.rn

# View generated bytecode
java -jar runescript.jar --emit-bytecode script.rn

# Start interactive REPL
java -jar runescript.jar
```

## Writing Your First Program

Create a file named `my_first_program.rn`:

```runescript
// Variables with explicit types
let name: string = "World";
let age: int = 25;
let is_student: bool = true;

// Print statements
print("Hello, ", name, "!");
print("Age: ", age);
print("Student: ", is_student);

// Arithmetic
let birth_year: int = 2026 - age;
print("Birth year: ", birth_year);

// Conditional
if (age >= 18) {
    print("Adult");
} else {
    print("Minor");
}

// Loop
let counter: int = 0;
while (counter < 3) {
    print("Count: ", counter);
    counter = counter + 1;
}
```

Run it:
```bash
java -jar runescript.jar my_first_program.rn
```

## Language Features Overview

### Types
- `int` - integers: `let x: int = 42;`
- `bool` - booleans: `let flag: bool = true;`
- `string` - text: `let msg: string = "Hello";`

### Variables
```runescript
let name: type = value;  // Declaration
name = new_value;        // Assignment
```

### Control Flow
```runescript
if (condition) {
    // code
} else {
    // alternative code
}

while (condition) {
    // loop body
}
```

### Unique Feature: Pipe Operator
```runescript
// Pass value as first argument to function
5 |> print();  // Same as print(5)

// Chain operations
let x: int = 10;
x |> add(5) |> multiply(2) |> print();  // print(multiply(add(x, 5), 2))
```

## Interactive Mode
Start REPL by running without arguments:
```bash
java -jar runescript.jar
```

Then type expressions directly:
```
> print("Hello");
Hello
Returned: null
> let x: int = 5;
> print(x);
5
Returned: null
> exit
```

## Troubleshooting

- **"no main manifest attribute"**: Make sure you're using `java -jar`
- **"Could not find or load main class"**: Check that runescript.jar is in the current directory
- **Syntax errors**: Check line numbers in error messages for the problematic code
